planned_actions: 1. Create src/cli/src/lib/envelope.ts: Define ENVELOPE_REGISTRY mapping artifact types to their const fields (version, artifact_type, producer_type) and artifact_id generation functions. Export injectEnvelope(artifactType, data, context) that sets undefined fields only (no-clobber).

2. Modify src/cli/src/commands/task.ts create action: Call injectEnvelope("task_contract", data, {task_id}) BEFORE validate() so injected const fields pass schema validation. Add status=drafted enforcement at command level (separate from envelope): if data.status is missing or not "drafted", force it to "drafted".
3. Modify src/cli/src/commands/mission.ts write-spec action: Call injectEnvelope("mission_spec", data, {mission_id}) BEFORE validate(). artifact_id rule: spec-{mission_id}.
4. Modify src/cli/src/commands/mission.ts write-brief action: Call injectEnvelope("design_brief", data, {mission_id}) BEFORE validate(). artifact_id rule: brief-{mission_id}.
5. Modify src/cli/src/commands/phase.ts write action: Call injectEnvelope("phase_review", data, {mission_phase, timestamp}) BEFORE validate(). artifact_id rule: phase-{mission_phase}-{timestamp}.
6. Do NOT modify evidence commands — trust boundary preserved.
7. Never inject created_at or updated_at — enrichTimestamp() owns those exclusively.
8. Build and bundle: cd src/cli && npm run build && npm run bundle
edge_cases: Agent explicitly passes version/artifact_type/producer_type — no-clobber must preserve their values
Agent passes status=ready on task create — must be overwritten to drafted (status enforcement is NOT no-clobber)
Agent passes artifact_id explicitly — no-clobber preserves it
phase write timestamp generation for artifact_id must match the timestamp used in the filename
Data with no task_id/mission_id context — injectEnvelope should handle missing context gracefully without crashing
Empty or null fields vs undefined — only inject when strictly undefined, not when null or empty string
non_goals: Modifying evidence add command or its force-overwrite pattern
Injecting created_at or updated_at (enrichTimestamp owns these)
Changing schema definitions — envelope values must already be valid per existing schemas
Adding envelope injection to read/list/transition commands
Refactoring existing validation or post-write-checks logic
demo_steps: AC1: Run geas task create with data missing version/artifact_type/producer_type — verify contract.json contains version=1.0, artifact_type=task_contract, producer_type=orchestration-authority, artifact_id=tc-{task_id}

AC2: Run geas mission write-spec with data missing envelope fields — verify spec.json contains version=1.0, artifact_type=mission_spec, artifact_id=spec-{mission_id}. Repeat for write-brief with artifact_type=design_brief, artifact_id=brief-{mission_id}
AC3: Run geas phase write with data missing envelope fields — verify phase review JSON contains version=1.0, artifact_type=phase_review, artifact_id=phase-{mission_phase}-{timestamp}
AC4: Run geas task create with explicit version=2.0 and artifact_type=custom — verify those values are preserved (no-clobber)
AC5: Run geas evidence add — verify no envelope injection occurs, existing behavior unchanged
AC6: Run geas task create with data containing artifact_id=my-custom-id — verify my-custom-id is preserved. Run without artifact_id — verify tc-{task_id} is generated
AC7: Verify enrichTimestamp still manages created_at exclusively — no created_at in envelope registry
AC8: Run geas task create with status=ready — verify output contract has status=drafted. Run with status omitted — verify status=drafted. Run with status=drafted — verify status=drafted

known_risks: phase_review artifact_id includes timestamp which varies per call - not idempotent
No-clobber means wrong explicit values pass through to schema validation, which is the intended behavior
untested_paths: mission write-spec with explicit envelope fields (no-clobber verify)
phase write with all fields explicitly provided
self_check.summary: All 8 ACs met. Build and bundle pass. Manual test verified task create auto-injects version, artifact_type, producer_type, artifact_id, status=drafted. enrichTimestamp owns created_at. Evidence command unchanged.

closure.change_summary: Created envelope.ts with ENVELOPE_REGISTRY for 4 artifact types. Modified task create, mission write-spec/write-brief, phase write to auto-inject version, artifact_type, producer_type, artifact_id before validation. status=drafted enforcement in task create. No-clobber semantics. Evidence excluded (trust boundary). enrichTimestamp owns timestamps.

reviews.summary: Registry pattern correct, injection before validation, evidence excluded
Auto-injection, no-clobber, status enforcement all verified

concerns.description: No-clobber semantics silently accept incorrect agent-supplied values. If an agent explicitly sends version=2.0 or artifact_type=wrong_type, no-clobber preserves these wrong values. Schema validation with const constraints WILL catch version and artifact_type (const: 1.0, const: task_contract), but producer_type is validated against agentType enum which includes all agent types -- so a worker agent could supply producer_type=qa-engineer instead of orchestration-authority and it would pass both no-clobber AND schema validation. This is a data integrity gap: the producer_type field becomes untrustworthy for audit purposes. Consider either force-overwriting producer_type like evidence does, or adding const constraints in schemas for producer_type where the correct value is known.

Registry covers only 4 of ~8 artifact types with envelope fields. debt-register, health-check, gap-assessment, rules-update, and recovery-packet all have envelope fields (version, artifact_type, artifact_id, producer_type) but are NOT in ENVELOPE_REGISTRY. These commands hardcode their envelope values inline (e.g., debt.ts:57-58, health.ts:374-375). This creates two divergent patterns for the same concern, increasing drift risk. The task contract scope was limited to 4 commands, so this is within scope -- but it is technical debt that should be tracked.

phase_review artifact_id includes a timestamp generated at injection time (new Date()) rather than using a deterministic value. If envelope injection were ever retried or if the artifact were regenerated, the artifact_id would change. All other artifact types use deterministic IDs (tc-{task_id}, spec-{mission_id}, brief-{mission_id}). The inconsistency means phase_review artifacts cannot be reliably correlated by ID across regeneration scenarios.

Status enforcement condition (line 112: if (!data.status || data.status !== drafted)) is logically redundant -- the falsy check is subsumed by the inequality check. More importantly, when an agent explicitly sends status=ready, the enforcement silently overwrites it without logging or warning. This is intentional per the contract, but silent correction of explicit agent input masks potential misunderstandings about the task lifecycle. A log message would aid debugging.

challenge_review.summary: Implementation is structurally sound. The registry pattern is clean, injection-before-validation ordering is correct, and the evidence trust boundary is properly maintained. Four non-blocking concerns identified: (1) producer_type no-clobber gap where wrong values can pass schema validation, (2) incomplete registry coverage creating two divergent envelope patterns, (3) non-deterministic artifact_id for phase_review, (4) silent status override without logging. None are blocking because schema const constraints catch the highest-risk fields (version, artifact_type), and the scope was explicitly limited to 4 commands. Recommend tracking concern #2 as technical debt.

verdict.rationale: All 8 ACs verified across gate tiers. Design authority approved both design and implementation review. QA independently confirmed auto-injection, no-clobber, and status enforcement. Challenger raised 4 non-blocking concerns -- producer_type gap, incomplete registry, non-deterministic phase ID, silent status override -- none of which represent correctness failures. DEBT-001 tracked for registry coverage. The feature solves the stated problem: agents no longer need to manually supply envelope fields, reducing errors and simplifying orchestration. Scope is faithful to contract with no creep. Backward compatibility preserved.

what_went_well: Registry pattern keeps envelope logic centralized
Injection-before-validation ordering is clean and correct
Challenger found real concerns that were properly scoped as non-blocking
what_was_surprising: producer_type validation gap - enum allows any agent type, not just the correct one. Schema const would be needed to fix this.
rule_candidates: When adding envelope auto-injection for new artifact types, add to ENVELOPE_REGISTRY in envelope.ts, not inline in command files
memory_candidates: envelope.ts ENVELOPE_REGISTRY is the single source for envelope defaults - 4 types covered, debt tracked for remaining
next_time_guidance: For high-risk tasks touching multiple files, challenger review catches real issues - always run it
